data.narrative:
Kepler Communications is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five same-sector, same-stage private companies (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace) and five public Space & Aerospace peers, providing directional anchors across the frontier-tech sector.

Two quantitative methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis, using the $200M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $340M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the median valuation across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables of $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B (Isar Aerospace, Skyryse floor) to $2.5B (Axiom Space ceiling). Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied because Kepler's revenue is not on file, limiting the utility of the 53.2x median P/S observed across public Space & Aerospace names.

The triangulated analysis across two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $289M to $1.7B, with a median of $920M. The wide spread reflects material tension between the stage-progression floor and the peer-median result. Key uncertainties include Kepler's revenue scale relative to the peer set and whether the company's competitive position and market opportunity warrant a valuation at or above the median comparable. Further financial disclosure would narrow this range substantially.
